title: Ensure 'Python version' is currently supported (if in use)

input_row.title: Ensure 'Python version' is currently supported (if in use)
input_row.description: Periodically, older versions of Python may be deprecated and no longer supported. Using a supported version of Python for App Service deployment slots is recommended to avoid potential unpatched vulnerabilities.
input_row.rationale: Deprecated and unsupported versions of programming and scripting languages can present vulnerabilities which may not be addressed or may not be addressable.
input_row.audit: Take note of the currently supported versions (given a status of "security") of Python here: https://devguide.python.org/versions/ Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings pane, ensure that for a Stack of Python, the Major version and Minor version reflect a currently supported release. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each app and deployment slot. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list apps: az webapp list For each app, run the following command to list deployment slots: az webapp deployment slot list --resource-group --name For each deployment slot, run the following command to get the Python version: az resource show --name web --resource-group -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type config --parent sites//slots/ --query properties.[linuxFxVersion,pythonVersion,windowsFxVersion] If Python is in use, ensure the version is currently supported. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 9c014953-ef68-4a98-82af-fd0f6b2306c8 - Name: 'App Service app slots that use Python should use a specified 'Python version''
input_row.remediation: Note: No action is required if Python is not in use. Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings pane, for a Stack of Python, set the Major version and Minor version to a currently supported release. 7. Click Save. 8. Click Continue. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each app and deployment slot requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI Run the following command to list supported runtimes: az webapp list-runtimes For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command with the appropriate parameters to update the Python version: az resource update --name web --resource-group -- namespace Microsoft.Web --resource-type config --parent sites//slots/ --set properties.[linuxFxVersion|windowsFxVersion]="PYTHON|"
input_row.impact: If your app is written using version-dependent features or libraries, they may not be available on more recent versions. If you wish to update, research the impact thoroughly.
input_row.default_value: The version is selected during creation.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access the AWS Management Console:**
   - Navigate to the AWS Management Console and log in.

2. **Identify the AWS Elastic Beanstalk or Lambda function (depending on your setup):**
   - For Elastic Beanstalk, go to the Elastic Beanstalk dashboard. 
   - For Lambda, navigate to the Lambda service.

3. **Locate your application or function:**
   - For Elastic Beanstalk: Click on the application name, then the environment where the deployment uses Python.
   - For Lambda: Click on the function you wish to audit.

4. **Verify the Python version being used:**
   - **For Elastic Beanstalk:**
     - Click on "Configuration" in the left navigation pane.
     - Under "Platform," check the Python version selected for your environment.
   - **For Lambda:**
     - In the function details, look for the "Runtime" field and ensure that the version of Python is an appropriate one (e.g., Python 3.8, 3.9, etc.) that is actively supported.

5. **Determine if the Python version is supported:**
   - Cross-reference the Python version with the list of currently supported versions from the [Python Developer's Guide on Versions](https://devguide.python.org/versions/). Ensure that the major and minor versions reflect those that are currently supported.

6. **Confirm compliance:**
   - If the Python version is not among the supported versions, it is non-compliant and needs to be updated.

**Expected secure configuration:**
- The application or function should be using a Python version listed as currently supported on the official Python website.

**How to confirm compliance:**
- Maintain a record of the checked versions and any required updates needed; compliance is achieved when each app or function runs an actively supported version of Python.
